events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed — system does not hold at target microns, indicating moisture, non-condensables, or a leak remaining in the refrigerant circuit",
    "Evacuation incomplete per manufacturer specs and industry standards; system not acceptable for refrigerant charge",
    "Brazed joints may have a pinhole or incomplete seal contributing to vacuum loss"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pressurize system with dry nitrogen and leak-test all brazed joints with electronic leak detector or soap solution",
    "Repair any leaking brazed joints by re-brazing with nitrogen purge flowing during brazing",
    "Re-evacuate system using a vacuum pump to achieve manufacturer-specified target microns (typically 500 microns or below)",
    "Perform vacuum decay test again — system must hold below target microns for minimum 15-30 minutes with no rise exceeding allowable threshold",
    "Do not charge system with refrigerant until evacuation is verified and decay test passes"
  ],
  "rationale": "A failed vacuum decay test at rough-complete stage means the system has not been properly evacuated and sealed. Per manufacturer specs, ACCA, and EPA 608 requirements, the system must achieve and hold a deep vacuum (typically 500 microns or less) before refrigerant charging. A system that cannot hold vacuum has a leak or moisture contamination that will compromise system performance, cause compressor damage, and violate code. The work is non-compliant and must be corrected before proceeding.",
  "references": ["mfr specs", "ACCA Standard 5 QI", "EPA 608", "ASHRAE 15"]
}
